meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Project Template: Solve Debugging Hurdles
content.seo_description: Master advanced debugging with our Antigravity project template. A student-focused guide to building robust apps using Claude Code and expert workflows.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't about trial and error; it's about system observability. By integrating the Antigravity template, you gain a structured environment that forces modularity. Use Claude Code to automate recursive error analysis, transforming your codebase from a tangled mess into a maintainable, high-performance architecture that scales alongside your skills.
content.intro:
The screen flickers, the terminal bleeds red text, and your momentum grinds to a halt. You’ve spent hours chasing a phantom bug, feeling the weight of the code you wrote yesterday becoming the barrier to your success tomorrow. This is the crucible every great engineer survives—the moment where frustration either breaks your spirit or births your breakthrough.

Enter the Antigravity project template. This isn't just boilerplate; it is a battle-tested architectural framework designed to isolate logic, enforce strict state management, and provide the diagnostic transparency required for complex AI-driven applications. We are moving beyond basic syntax errors into the realm of professional-grade systems engineering.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Framework
steps.description: Establish the foundational directory structure that separates concerns and isolates business logic from UI components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity template with strict TypeScript configuration and modular architecture.

steps.title: Inject Observability Hooks
steps.description: Configure automated logging and state snapshots to ensure you can trace data flow before a crash occurs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add instrumentation hooks to the main service layer that capture state snapshots on every function call.

steps.title: Automated Error Isolation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a recursive trace of the current failure point within the template.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the stack trace in the terminal and perform a root cause analysis on the current module.

steps.title: Refactor via Dependency Injection
steps.description: Decouple your logic to make unit testing and debugging significantly easier as the project grows.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current service to use dependency injection for all external data sources to improve testability.

steps.title: Validate with Synthetic Tests
steps.description: Create automated test suites that simulate the edge cases causing your current debugging roadblocks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests covering the edge cases identified in the recent error logs.

pro_tips: Always treat your error logs as data; pipe them into a local JSON file to let Claude Code perform trend analysis on failure patterns.
Use the 'Atomic Commit' rule: never fix a bug without writing a corresponding regression test first.
Leverage the Antigravity 'hot-reload' diagnostic mode to see variable state changes in real-time without stopping the process.
If your prompt fails to solve the bug, ask Claude Code to 'Explain the architectural constraint' preventing the fix rather than asking for the code directly.
Document your debugging journey in a 'Decision Log' file; future employers value the narrative of how you solved hard problems more than the code itself.
content.next_steps: Once your environment is stable, begin refactoring your legacy modules into the Antigravity pattern and document your performance gains in your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the framework to turn your debugging nightmares into production-ready features. Stop fighting the code and start architecting the future. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
